name: Foz de Biniés and Foz de Fago Loop
name_original: Las dos Foz: Foz de Biniés y Foz de Fago — vuelta desde Ansó
description: This difficult 35.5-mile touring cycling route takes you through the Foz de Biniés and Foz de Fago gorges, returning from Ansó.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Ansó are situated within the Aragonese Pyrenees, offering diverse terrain for cyclists. The region is characterized by high mountains, lush valleys like Ansó and Hecho, and dense forests of beeches, pines, and firs. Dramatic gorges, such as Foces de Fago y Biniés, and crystalline rivers also define the landscape, providing varied backdrops for cycling. Best touring cycling routes around Ansó The most popular touring cycling route is Foz de Biniés and Foz de Fago Loop, a 35.5 miles (57.1 km) trail that takes 4 hours 4 minutes to complete. This difficult route traverses dramatic gorges, offering challenging ascents and descents. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Ansó Village – Selva de Oza Valley loop from Ansó, a difficult 44.6 miles (71.8 km) path. This route leads through the lush Selva de Oza natural area, surrounded by mountains and a river. Local touring cyclists also love the View of Agua Tuertas Valley – Selva de Oza Valley loop from GR11 & GR65.3.3, an 11.1 miles (17.8 km) trail leading through the tranquil Aguas Tuertas Valley with its meandering crystalline waters, often completed in about 1 hour 33 minutes. Touring cycling around Ansó is defined by high Pyrenean mountains, lush valleys, and dense forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, including 1 easy, 11 moderate, and 52 difficult routes. The routes in Ansó are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 1300 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Ansó's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Ansó?
A: Ansó offers a wide variety of touring cycling routes, with over 60 options available. These routes cater to different skill levels, including 1 easy, 11 moderate, and 52 difficult trails, ensuring there's something for every cyclist.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Ansó?
A: The terrain around Ansó is incredibly diverse, ranging from lush valleys and dense forests of beeches, pines, and firs, to dramatic gorges and high Pyrenean mountains. You'll encounter challenging ascents and descents, quiet roads, and picturesque paths alongside crystalline rivers, all offering magnificent views.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Ansó?
A: While many routes in Ansó are challenging, there is at least one easy touring cycling route available. This allows beginners to experience the beautiful landscapes of the Aragonese Pyrenees at a more relaxed pace. For more options, consider exploring the 11 moderate routes as your skills progress.

Q: What are some challenging touring cycling routes in Ansó?
A: For experienced touring cyclists seeking a challenge, Ansó offers numerous difficult routes. One such route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24570422" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva del Ibón – Laza Pass (1,129 m) loop from GR 11 / GRT 13 / GR 11.4&lt;/a&gt;, which spans over 75 km with significant elevation gain. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38167487" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ansó – Chinebral de Gamueta loop from Zotarola&lt;/a&gt;, covering over 42 km with nearly 1000 meters of ascent.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while cycling in Ansó?
A: Ansó's touring cycling routes are rich with natural beauty and historical landmarks. You can explore the dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e979929051/foz-de-binies-and-foz-de-fago-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Foces de Fago y Biniés&lt;/a&gt;, cycle through the lush &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15008569" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Selva de Oza natural area&lt;/a&gt;, or discover the tranquil &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38785021" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aguas Tuertas Valley&lt;/a&gt; with its meandering waters and megalithic remains. Additionally, you might encounter highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3141925" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ansabère Huts&lt;/a&gt; or the stunning &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/307857" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lac Gentau and View of Pic du Midi d'Ossau&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there circular touring cycling routes in Ansó?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes around Ansó are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e979929051/foz-de-binies-and-foz-de-fago-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Foz de Biniés and Foz de Fago Loop&lt;/a&gt;, the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15008569" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ansó Village – Selva de Oza Valley loop&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38785021" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Agua Tuertas Valley – Selva de Oza Valley loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Ansó?
A: The best time for touring cycling in Ansó is generally from spring through autumn. During these seasons, the weather is more favorable, and the mountain passes are typically clear of snow. While winter offers opportunities for cross-country skiing and snowshoeing in areas like Linza, many cycling routes may be inaccessible or require specialized equipment due to snow and ice.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Ansó?
A: The touring cycling routes in Ansó are highly regarded by the komoot community, boasting an average rating of 4.8 stars from over 40 reviews. Cyclists frequently praise the stunning Pyrenean landscapes, the variety of terrain, and the opportunity to explore pristine natural areas like the Valles Occidentales Natural Park.

Q: Are there family-friendly touring cycling routes in Ansó?
A: While many routes are difficult, there is at least one easy touring cycling route in Ansó that could be suitable for families. When planning a family outing, look for routes with minimal elevation gain and shorter distances. The tranquil Aguas Tuertas Valley, for example, offers a relatively flat and scenic experience.

Q: Can I bring my dog on touring cycling routes in Ansó?
A: The Aragonese Pyrenees, including the Ansó region, are known for their rich biodiversity and protected natural areas. While many trails are generally accessible, it's always best to check specific regulations for each route or protected park (like Valles Occidentales Natural Park) regarding dogs, especially concerning leash requirements and restricted areas to protect wildlife.

Q: Where can I find parking for touring cycling routes in Ansó?
A: The village of Ansó, being a charming base for outdoor activities, typically offers parking facilities. You can usually find designated parking areas within or on the outskirts of the village, providing convenient access to the start points of many touring cycling routes. It's advisable to arrive early during peak season.

Q: Is it possible to reach the touring cycling routes in Ansó by public transport?
A: While Ansó is a somewhat remote village nestled in the Pyrenees, there are often limited public transport options, such as bus services, connecting it to larger towns in the region. However, direct public transport to the specific trailheads of touring cycling routes might be infrequent or unavailable. It's recommended to check local bus schedules in advance or consider driving to the starting points.

Q: What cultural attractions can I visit in Ansó after my ride?
A: After your touring cycling adventure, you can explore the charming medieval village of Ansó itself, recognized as one of Spain's most beautiful villages. Visit the Ansó Costume Museum to see traditional dress, explore the 16th-century Medieval Tower, or discover the Ethnology Museum and the historic San Pedro Church for insights into local life and art.
tips: In La Jacetania, municipality of Ansó, we find a stretch of about 3 km in length of great scenic value, with several easy accesses to the riverbed. The Foz de Biniés is listed as a Point of Geological Interest in Aragon. The crystal-clear waters of the Veral River , from Zuriza to its mouth in the Aragón river, present exceptional natural values, with still virgin river stretches. The section proposed here is a walking tour of the Foz de Biniés, a must for all nature lovers in Huesca.
Spectacular town as it is all located on a high hill from the rest of the Aragón river valley. From afar you can see its location and where it is in the western end of the province of Huesca.

The Foz de Fago is very close to the town of Fago. Gorge canyon very well excavated and with all kinds of attractions, jumps, siphons, waterfalls that force us to rappel... It is famous for its descents in good times, with equipped material and technique. From the road you can see the narrow sections of the river.

A beautiful medieval village with traditional Pyrenean architecture. Visit the 16th-century medieval tower, the Virgen de Puyeta hermitage, the San Pedro church, and the museums. There are also several restaurants where you can enjoy typical local cuisine.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is classified as 'difficult' and requires 'very good fitness'. Cyclists should be prepared for significant climbs and descents over its 57.1-kilometer distance and 1,200 meters of ascent.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this touring bicycle route?
faqs.answer: The route is primarily on paved surfaces, offering a smooth ride for much of its length. However, be aware that some sections may be unpaved and difficult, potentially requiring you to push your bike.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll cycle through stunning natural landscapes and charming villages. Key highlights include the dramatic Foz de Biniés and Foz de Fago gorges, as well as the picturesque settlements of Berdún and Ansó.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The region around Huesca, where this route is located, offers diverse landscapes. Generally, spring and autumn provide the most pleasant cycling conditions with milder temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summer can be very hot, especially in the gorges.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is a loop returning to Ansó, so parking in or near Ansó would be the most convenient option for starting and finishing your ride.

faqs.question: Is public transport available to reach the starting point of the route?
faqs.answer: While specific public transport details for cyclists to Ansó are not provided, it's advisable to check local bus services connecting to larger towns in the Jacetania region if you plan to use public transport.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: This route passes through the Paisaje Protegido de las Foces de Fago y Biniés. Generally, there are no specific entrance fees or permits required for cycling through protected landscapes in Aragon, but always respect local regulations and signage regarding access and conservation.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are often allowed in natural areas, this is a challenging touring bicycle route. Consider if the distance, elevation, and potential unpaved sections are suitable for your dog, especially if they would need to run alongside or be carried. Always keep dogs on a leash and clean up after them.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay along the route?
faqs.answer: The route passes through villages like Berdún and Ansó, which are likely to offer options for food, drinks, and accommodation. It's always a good idea to check opening hours in advance, especially outside of peak season.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling or hiking trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other paths. You'll find sections that overlap with the Viejo Camino de Santiago por el Valle de Hecho - Calzada del Summo Pyreneo, Sendero Prepirenaico, Ruta Transpirenaica en BTT (Aisa - Roncesvalles), and various segments of the GR-15.

faqs.question: What essential gear should I bring for this touring bicycle route?
faqs.answer: Given the route's difficulty and length, essential gear includes a well-maintained touring bicycle, helmet, appropriate cycling attire, sufficient water and snacks, a repair kit, first-aid supplies, and navigation tools. Be prepared for varied terrain and potential weather changes in the Pyrenees.

faqs.question: What should I know about the challenging sections of the route?
faqs.answer: While much of the route is paved, be prepared for some unpaved and difficult sections. These parts may require you to dismount and push your bike, adding to the challenge. Ensure your bike is suitable for varied terrain and that you have the fitness for these demanding stretches.
